idea_title: Calibration versus accuracy tradeoff in medical pseudo-label selection

selected_idea.title: Calibration versus accuracy tradeoff in medical pseudo-label selection
selected_idea.pitch: Uncertainty-aware methods often optimize accuracy-like metrics, while medical deployment needs calibrated confidence. The gap is to measure whether pseudo-label filters improve accuracy while preserving calibration under noisy labels.
selected_idea.mechanism: Adding entropy and agreement constraints will improve accuracy without increasing expected calibration error compared with raw confidence pseudo-labeling.
selected_idea.testable_hypothesis: Adding entropy and agreement constraints will improve accuracy without increasing expected calibration error compared with raw confidence pseudo-labeling.
selected_idea.required_dataset: controlled SSL proxy with noisy labels and calibration metrics; remote follow-up: MedMNIST with ECE/Brier evaluation.
selected_idea.baseline_to_beat: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics

topic: uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection for semi-supervised medical image classification under label noise
label: Micro-Probe
hypothesis: Adding entropy and agreement constraints will improve accuracy without increasing expected calibration error compared with raw confidence pseudo-labeling.
method: Adding entropy and agreement constraints will improve accuracy without increasing expected calibration error compared with raw confidence pseudo-labeling.
baseline: [EV001] Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics
dataset: controlled SSL proxy with noisy labels and calibration metrics; remote follow-up: MedMNIST with ECE/Brier evaluation.
expected_result: The proposed method (Calibration versus accuracy tradeoff in medical pseudo-label selection) should beat the baseline on the specified metric.
failure_interpretation: A weak or negative margin means the mechanism does not justify promotion and should be revised or killed.
